name: Datadog API
what_it_does: Datadog's REST API for querying metrics, logs, traces, and events, managing monitors and alerts, and automating infrastructure observability workflows.
use_cases: Querying real-time and historical metrics for agent-driven analysis
Creating and managing monitors and alert policies programmatically
Searching and analyzing logs from agent workflows
Triggering incident creation and management via agents
Building custom dashboards and SLO tracking automation
not_for: Teams not using Datadog (high cost for new adoption)
Small projects with minimal observability needs
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Datadog's rich observability data for AIOps, automated incident response, or infrastructure health monitoring.
avoid_when: You don't already use Datadog, or cost is a major constraint.

auth.notes: Two-key system: API key + Application key. API key authenticates the source, Application key scopes permissions. Both required for most operations. Keys are per-organization.

pricing.paid_tiers: Infrastructure from $15/host/month, APM custom, Logs based on ingestion
pricing.notes: One of the more expensive observability platforms. Pricing per host for infrastructure, per volume for logs and APM. Costs scale rapidly with infrastructure size.

performance.rate_limits: 300 requests/hour for metrics API, 1,000 requests/hour for other APIs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured error responses with errors array containing detailed messages. HTTP status codes are semantically correct. Rate limit errors include rate limit headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Monitor creation/updates use ID-based idempotency. Metric submissions are naturally idempotent (last-write-wins for gauges). Event submissions are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Two-key authentication (API key + App key) is a common agent configuration mistake
Metrics API rate limits are strict — 300/hour can be exhausted by frequent polling
Log query syntax (Datadog Query Language) is custom and must be learned
Different API versions (v1, v2) have different capabilities — v2 preferred for new integrations
Metric names require specific formatting (dot-separated, snake_case)
Dashboard and monitor IDs are numeric — require lookup before operations

security_score_components.security_notes: Dual-key auth (API key + Application key) adds security layer. Application keys scoped with permissions. SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, FedRAMP. No MCP server — agents use REST API or official SDKs.
